problem_statement: Convert the uploaded Spark Platform Complete Build Specification v2 and complete ADL set into a repo-grounded Spark Genesis lifecycle planning package for AKTI Spark Core v0_0_2. The package must prepare reviewed control planning for Train 1 Levels 1-4 only, without creating tickets, seed matrix, implementation code, deployment, production secret access, or Train 2+ authorization.
primary_users: Spark Genesis planning and readiness workflow
business_outcome: Establish committed planning authority for Spark Genesis to evaluate and prepare AKTI Spark Platform Build v2 lifecycle readiness, with Level 1 cloud infrastructure and deployment as the first bounded train and explicit human review gates before any ticket pack, seed matrix, implementation, production deployment, or later-level train is authorized.
scope: Commit the uploaded Spark Platform Complete Build Specification v2 as planning input at docs/process/core/v0_0_2/spark_plan_v2.md.
Commit the uploaded complete ADL bundle as read-only planning constraints at docs/process/core/v0_0_2/spark_adl_complete.md.
Commit this updated Spark Platform Build v2 phase intent packet as lifecycle planning authority.
Record the eight accepted human decisions for Genesis lifecycle planning.
Run Spark Genesis v0.3.5 project-aware validation, intent readiness, clarification, and lifecycle init checks.
Create a Genesis lifecycle planning report for human review using the confirmed Train 1 Levels 1-4 boundary.
Prepare only the next recommended artifact path for Train 1 Levels 1-4 control planning; do not create it in this task.
non_scope: No Train 2+ ticket generation or authorization before Train 1 Levels 1-4 control outputs/readiness are approved.
No Prisma schema or migration changes.
No desktop, mobile, or P2P first-train tickets.
required_screens: No frontend screen work is authorized by this lifecycle planning package.

Future frontend work requires approved screen contracts defining route, purpose, users, required capabilities, actions, data source, fields, validation, states, mobile behavior, audit hooks, and prohibited behavior.
required_apis_data_sources: local git status and HEAD for AKTI Spark
capabilities_permissions: No new capabilities, permissions, roles, APIs, events, screens, or business rules are authorized by this packet.
Future tickets must derive capabilities and permissions from committed contracts, manifests, Access Core, Gatekeeper, and approved control docs.
data_ownership: No data model ownership changes are authorized by this packet.
Future tenant-scoped data changes must follow Prisma, RLS, organization_id, index, and negative cross-org test requirements from AKTI authority.
integrations_adapters: Cloudflare remains the intended edge/WAF/CDN/DNS layer where infrastructure planning requires edge specificity.
AWS EKS is the locked Level 1 Kubernetes provider assumption for planning.
No production credentials, real external adapter activation, production WhatsApp, or production deployment is authorized.
ai_data_boundary: No runtime AI integration is authorized by this packet.
Future AI-readable/prohibited data boundaries must be explicitly defined in committed control docs before implementation.
validation_expectations: Uploaded Spark plan is committed under lower_snake_case process path.
Uploaded ADL bundle is committed under lower_snake_case process path and all affected ADL statuses remain ACCEPTED.
Spark Genesis project config validation returns no STOP findings.
Spark Genesis autonomous preflight returns no STOP findings.
Spark Genesis intent readiness returns PASS for this packet.
Spark Genesis lifecycle init writes only under ignored codex-review/core/v0_0_2/spark_platform_build_v2_genesis_lifecycle_planning/.
Only approved docs/process files are changed.
acceptance_criteria: Committed planning files use lower_snake_case paths.
Eight human decisions are encoded in this packet and in the human decisions record.
Train 1 Levels 1-4 is the first train boundary and Train 2+ ticket generation is not authorized.
Production deployment and secrets access remain locked out until explicit human approval.
Desktop/mobile/P2P first-train scope is deferred until cloud is stable and beta tested.
Ticket Quality Doctrine enforcement lines are present.
Genesis lifecycle planning report recommends docs/process/core/v0_0_2/spark_platform_build_v2_train_1_l1_l4_control_plan_v1.md as the next artifact and does not create it.
No tickets, seed matrix, implementation code, deployment, or Spark Genesis repository changes were created.
stop_conditions: Stop if missing or conflicting repo authority is found.
Stop if any affected ADL status is not ACCEPTED.
Stop if Train 2+ ticket generation is attempted before Train 1 Levels 1-4 approval.
Stop if production deployment is attempted.
Stop if production secrets access is attempted.
Stop if desktop/mobile/P2P tickets are attempted in the first train.
Stop if implementation code is attempted.
Stop if ticket pack creation is attempted before lifecycle planning approval.
Stop if seed matrix creation is attempted before explicit approval.
Stop if frontend screen work is attempted without approved screen contracts.
Stop if stale, vague, shallow, overlapping, unsafe, or non-predictive ticket quality would be produced.
Stop if a runtime/frontend/backend/schema/generated/package/lockfile file would need to change.
Stop if Spark Genesis repo changes would be required.
Stop if lifecycle artifacts write outside ignored codex-review/core/v0_0_2/spark_platform_build_v2_genesis_lifecycle_planning/.
human_decisions_still_needed: Human approval of docs/process/core/v0_0_2/spark_platform_build_v2_train_1_l1_l4_control_plan_v1.md before Train 1 Levels 1-4 ticket pack or seed matrix creation.
Human approval before production deployment, production credentials, exact production account values, or region-specific production configuration.
Human approval before Train 2+ planning or ticket generation.

genesis_role.authority_boundary: Genesis does not decide whether execution is one long run or level-bounded trains. Genesis informs readiness/risk/ticket quality. Human confirms execution train decisions.

enforcement_lines: Implementation is not stale by itself. Tickets become stale.
Apply maximum concrete capability within the approved scope of each ticket.
ticket_quality_doctrine.risk_definition: The risk is stale, vague, shallow, overlapping, unsafe, or non-predictive tickets.

level_1_infrastructure_assumption.region_and_account_values: deployment configuration only; not hardcoded repo values

uploaded_inputs.role: complete build specification; Genesis generates tickets from this plan constrained by ADLs
Architecture Decision Log constraints; read-only planning authority

required_before_level_4: ADL-002 Event Versioning and Schema Registry
required_before_level_8: ADL-014 Refund to Original Payment Method
required_before_level_12: ADL-023 Waitlist Auto-Promotion with Claim Deadline
adl_constraints.adl_rule: Read ADLs as constraints before generating tickets for affected levels. If any ADL status is PROPOSED or unresolved, block affected level and surface for human decision. Do not implement directly from ADL files.

level_plan_reference.level_1: part of Train 1 Levels 1-4 after lifecycle planning review
level_plan_reference.level_2_through_level_18: Train 1 includes Levels 2-4 only; Levels 5-18 are future train candidates and are not authorized for ticket generation by this packet.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.name: Cloud Infrastructure and Deployment
preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Kubernetes/container platform, managed Postgres, object storage, CDN, API gateway, CI/CD, observability, DNS, backups, runbooks, feature flags infrastructure.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.name: Storage and Sync
preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: SVFS, cloud sync, object storage, image processing, backups, soft/staged deletion, encryption, P2P deferred/optional.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: MFA, sessions, org/branch identity, user/role/API key management, access revocation, TOS, consent logging.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Event Bus, Notification Engine, Communication Gateway, Search, File Service, AI Proxy, Audit Log, Webhooks, API Gateway.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Workflow Builder, Lifecycle Builder, Rules Engine, Form Builder, AI Configuration Wizard, Template Library, Custom Fields provider interfaces.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Product catalogue, pricing, batches, discount engine, inventory, multi-currency display.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.name: CRM
preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Lead intake, lead/contact records, WhatsApp/email engines, pipeline, scoring, follow-up, companies/contacts, reports.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.name: Finance
preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Invoicing, collections, payments, expenses, GL, banking/reconciliation, reports, payroll, platform billing.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.name: HR
preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Employee records, org structure, recruitment, attendance, leave, performance, commission, policies/offboarding.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.name: Workspace
preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Messaging, tasks/projects, documents/knowledge base, calendar/meetings, announcements/reminders.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.name: LMS
preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Student lifecycle, timetable, attendance, assignments, assessments, content, portals, certificates, university-scale extensions.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Event lifecycle, multi-track/session, registration/ticketing, waitlist, check-in, post-event CRM/reporting.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Email/WhatsApp/SMS campaigns, Meta Ads, sequences, push notifications, attribution.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.name: E-Commerce
preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Stores, product listing, cart/checkout, payments, orders, shipping, returns/refunds, optional marketplace.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.name: Website and App Builder
preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Visual editor, blocks, multi-site, SEO/blog, PWA builder.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.name: AI Business Consultant
preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Individual intelligence, prediction maturity, after-action reviews, health dashboard, collective intelligence, hard AI limits.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.name: Admin, Onboarding, and Support
preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: Org/admin tools, data migration, AI Concierge onboarding, support, documentation, compliance tools.

preserved_uploaded_level_plan.name: Design System and Frontend
preserved_uploaded_level_plan.scope_summary: 18a design tokens before frontend, 18b component library, 18c-18r module frontends after backend stability and contracts.

cross_cutting_requirements_reference: Custom fields system across all modules/entities.
Multi-account and multi-branch external integration model.
Fluid workflow architecture: configurable business rules, no hardcoded operator-specific logic.
Global opt-out registry enforced at Communication Gateway.
Idempotency for write APIs and event consumers.
UTC storage, user timezone display, localization/i18n and RTL architecture.
Security layer: infra security, app security, security ops, compliance.
Redundancy and failure modes: database, object storage, event bus, Kubernetes, external services, DNS, CI/CD fallback, human redundancy.
Testing strategy: unit/integration/e2e/security/performance and test-mode isolation.
Module sandbox and dry-run data isolation.
